Bug / Task Description: astar returns inflated distances when a non-trivial admissible heuristic is supplied. With a zero heuristic it looks fine, but with a real heuristic the recorded distances (and hence the found cost) are larger than the true shortest-path cost — the heuristic is being double-counted into accumulated distance.
Commit Subject: fix(shortest_path): accumulate A* g-score from cost-so-far, not priority f-value
